title: Malapoenna japonica
intro:
There is no information available on "Malapoenna japonica." 

However, based on available information, I will make an educated guess and suggest that it might be a typo or confusion with "Malpighia", specifically Malpighia glabra (also known as Jamaican cherry or Panama berry).

Malpighia glabra is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, including the Caribbean. It is a shrub or small tree with a wide range of health benefits, including its potential to help lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant.

The bioactive constituents of Malpighia glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties and are often extracted and used in herbal preparations.

The most common herbal preparations made with Malpighia glabra include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and as a natural remedy for various ailments.
